summary: Iteration 4 (vendor + player + social settings) tested end-to-end. Created new pytest file test_vendor_player_settings.py (29 tests). Combined regression: 81 passed / 12 skipped across 4 test files. TWO CRITICAL BACKEND BUGS FOUND that block the entire player-profile system end-to-end: (1) auto-generated email 'player_<mobile>@playsphere.local' fails Pydantic EmailStr validation -> POST /api/players/register returns 500 whenever the optional email field is omitted (the frontend PlayerSignup form has NO email field per design, so EVERY UI signup fails — reproduced in browser: toast 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'). (2) Route-ordering bug: the legacy roster routes GET/PATCH /api/players/{player_id} are registered (line ~787) BEFORE the new GET /api/players/me, GET /api/players/profiles, PATCH /api/players/me (line ~1303+). FastAPI matches in registration order, so /players/me is captured by /players/{player_id} and returns 'Player not found' (404 with detail='Player not found' confirmed via curl). PATCH /api/players/me hits the old admin-only roster patch and returns 403 'Admin only'. Result: even if bug #1 is fixed, the profile endpoints are unreachable. Both bugs together => /players/signup, /players/me, /players/profiles are fully broken. NOTE: the main agent's request notes said legacy roster was moved to /team-players/:id on the FRONTEND, but the BACKEND was not renamed. Everything else verified working: /api/companies/public (no auth), /api/settings GET (public) + PATCH (platform_admin only, 403 for company_admin), /api/vendors/signup + /me + listings (vendor_type inherited, approved=false by default, self-approve attempt ignored, owner-only mutations), /api/vendor-listings (vendor_id stripped from public response confirmed, ?vendor_type/city filters work), /api/admin/listings + approve flow (unapproved listings hidden from /vendor-listings until admin approves), /api/vendor-bookings full lifecycle (company_admin creates pending booking on approved listing, price/currency copied, vendor sees own bookings and can confirm/decline, company_admin can cancel own, listing-not-approved => 404). Frontend verified: /platform-admin Settings tab — all 5 setting-{facebook,instagram,linkedin,twitter,youtube}_url inputs + settings-save present; save triggers 'Saved' toast and persists; footer correctly renders ONLY icons whose URLs are set (saved facebook+instagram => 2 icons in footer; others hidden). pa-tab-vendors shows 5 approve buttons (pa-approve-vendor-*), pa-tab-listings shows 2 approve buttons. Demo vendor (ravi@turf.in) approved with 1 listing 'Whitefield Premium Turf' INR 2500 visible in /vendor-listings.

critical.issue: Auto-generated email player_<mobile>@playsphere.local fails EmailStr; returns 500. Frontend form has no email field => 100% of UI signups fail.
Shadowed by legacy /api/players/{player_id} registered earlier in server.py. Returns 'Player not found' (404) or 'Admin only' (403). Player profile system completely unreachable via API.

ui_bugs.issue: Form submission fails with backend 500 (see backend critical #1); toast 'Something went wrong. Please try again.' appears.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot reach /players/me even if signup succeeded because backend route is shadowed. Profile editor and Find Players will not work.

action_items: Backend fix (Critical #1): in POST /api/players/register, either store body.email as None when not provided and relax UserPublic.email to Optional[EmailStr]/Optional[str], or use a valid TLD for the auto-generated email (e.g. f'player_{mobile}@players.playsphere.com'). PydanticEmailStr deems '.local' a reserved special-use TLD.

Backend fix (Critical #2): remove or rename the legacy /api/players/{player_id} GET/PATCH/DELETE handlers (lines ~780-815 in server.py) to /api/team-players/{player_id} to match the frontend route rename. Alternatively re-order so /players/me and /players/profiles are declared BEFORE /players/{player_id}. Currently FastAPI matches by registration order, so /players/me returns 'Player not found' and PATCH /players/me returns 403 'Admin only'.

Once both bugs are fixed, flip the assertion in test_register_without_email_should_succeed_but_fails_due_to_local_tld_bug from 500 -> 200, and remove the pytestmark skip on TestPlayers so the 12 currently-skipped tests run.
critical_code_review_comments: Route ordering bug is a textbook FastAPI pitfall: any literal path that overlaps a path-parameter should be declared before the parameter route or use an explicit prefix. Recommend moving every new /players/* route into its own APIRouter mounted under a different prefix (e.g. /api/player-accounts) to permanently isolate it from the roster CRUD.

server.py is now 2100 lines. The player/vendor/settings additions push it past the maintainable threshold. Strongly suggest splitting into routers/players.py, routers/vendors.py, routers/settings.py, routers/roster.py.
EmailStr is used widely (UserPublic, VendorSignupBody). Any default-generated email must use a real TLD. Consider centralising a make_internal_email(prefix, id) helper.

PATCH /api/players/me strips 'view_count' but does not strip 'created_at' / 'company_name' (company_name is overwritten only when company_id changes). A player could PATCH company_name to a spoofed string when not changing company_id.

VendorListingCreate accepts 'active': bool from client; combined with vendor self-PATCH that strips 'approved' but not 'active', a vendor could leave a listing active=True. Acceptable behaviour but document it.
PATCH /api/vendors/me/listings strips approved but does not strip vendor_type — a vendor could change vendor_type after creation. Recommend stripping it too.

GET /api/vendor-bookings for vendor falls back to flt={'vendor_id':'__none__'} when vendor record missing — silent empty list. Should raise 404 'Vendor not registered' instead so frontend can show the right message.
Idempotent vendor signup is missing — re-signing up with the same email returns 400, but there is no /api/vendors/login path; vendors must use /api/auth/login. Doc this in test_credentials.md.
/api/settings PATCH does not validate URLs. Malicious admin could store javascript: URL that Footer would render as href. Add simple http(s)://-only validator.

success_rate.frontend: Player flows 0% (blocked by backend); Settings/Footer/Platform-Admin tabs 100%

test_credentials: admin@playsphere.com/admin123 (platform_admin) verified; acme@example.com/acme123 (company_admin) verified; ravi@turf.in/vendor123 (vendor) verified; viewer@playsphere.com/viewer123 not exercised.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_ prefixed records during testing: 2 TEST_ vendors (TEST_Biz_*, TEST_Biz2_*), a few TEST_ listings (auto-deleted in test bodies; TEST_NoSelfApprove_* and TEST_Owner_* are deleted), 1 cancelled TEST vendor-booking on Whitefield Premium Turf. Two TEST_ vendors remain (not deleted as approval-flip test only flipped state). No production data modified. Player-side: 0 records created because of the EmailStr bug.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Once main agent fixes BOTH backend bugs (1) EmailStr/.local in POST /api/players/register and (2) move/rename legacy /api/players/{player_id} so it does not shadow /api/players/me & /api/players/profiles: (a) flip the assertion in test_register_without_email_should_succeed_but_fails_due_to_local_tld_bug from 500 -> 200, (b) remove the pytestmark skip on TestPlayers. The 12 skipped tests cover: duplicate-mobile-400, player login success+wrong-password, GET /players/me, PATCH /players/me (full field set), PATCH does-not-leak mobile/view_count/user_id/id, change-company flow, mobile masking on /players/profiles list, ?q= search, view_count increment on cross-user view + non-increment on self-view, ?company_id= filter. Frontend ground-truth: PlayerSignup has no email input — fix must accept signup without email. Demo vendor already has 1 approved listing ('Whitefield Premium Turf' Bangalore INR 2500/hour) — use it for /hire flow + vendor booking lifecycle without creating fresh data.
rca of the issue: Bug #1 RCA: server.py line 1264 builds `email = (body.email or f'player_{body.mobile}@playsphere.local').lower()` and at line 1289 returns `UserPublic(... email=email ...)`. UserPublic.email is `EmailStr` (Pydantic v2 email-validator), which rejects '.local' as a 'special-use or reserved name'. Validation raises pydantic ValidationError -> FastAPI 500. Reproduced via curl + via UI (PlayerSignup form has no email input). Fix: either change UserPublic.email to `Optional[str]`, OR use a routable TLD like '@players.playsphere.com' for the fallback. Bug #2 RCA: server.py registers `@api.get('/players/{player_id}')` at line 787 (legacy roster) BEFORE `@api.get('/players/me')` at line 1303 and `@api.get('/players/profiles')` at line 1330. FastAPI/Starlette walks routes in registration order and matches the first regex hit, so '/players/me' is captured as player_id='me' and the legacy lookup returns 404 'Player not found'. PATCH /players/me hits the legacy admin-only PATCH handler at line 802 -> 403 'Admin only'. Fix options: (A) move all `/players/*` new routes ABOVE the `/players/{player_id}` route, OR (B) rename legacy roster routes to `/team-players/{player_id}` to match the frontend rename mentioned in the problem statement (the spec actually says 'Existing roster player detail moved from /players/:id to /team-players/:id', so renaming the backend routes is the intended fix that was missed).
